title: Cisco DHCP Server
description: Learn to configure a Cisco IOS router as a DHCP server, including address pools, excluded ranges, default gateways, DNS servers, lease times, and DHCP relay with helper addresses.

tasks.prompt: Exclude IP addresses 192.168.1.1 through 192.168.1.10 from the DHCP pool.
tasks.explanation: Excluded addresses reserve specific IPs for static assignments such as gateways, servers, and printers so DHCP does not lease them.

tasks.prompt: Create a DHCP address pool named 'LAN-POOL'.
tasks.explanation: The 'ip dhcp pool' command enters DHCP pool configuration mode where you define the network scope and options for clients.

tasks.prompt: Define the DHCP network as 192.168.1.0 with subnet mask 255.255.255.0.
tasks.explanation: The 'network' command specifies the subnet from which the DHCP server assigns addresses to requesting clients.

tasks.prompt: Set the default gateway (default-router) for DHCP clients to 192.168.1.1.
tasks.explanation: The default-router option (Option 3) tells DHCP clients which IP address to use as their default gateway.

tasks.prompt: Configure the DNS server option for DHCP clients to 8.8.8.8.
tasks.explanation: The dns-server option (Option 6) provides clients with a DNS resolver address for hostname lookups.

tasks.prompt: Set the DHCP domain name option to 'corp.local'.
tasks.explanation: The domain-name option (Option 15) appends a default DNS suffix to unqualified hostnames on client machines.

tasks.prompt: Set the DHCP lease time to 7 days, 0 hours, and 0 minutes.
tasks.explanation: The lease command controls how long a client may use an assigned IP address before it must renew or release it.

tasks.prompt: Configure interface GigabitEthernet0/0 as a DHCP relay by forwarding broadcasts to DHCP server 10.0.0.5.
tasks.explanation: The ip helper-address command converts local DHCP broadcasts into unicast packets forwarded to a remote DHCP server across subnets.
